channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: An Australia police officer who shocked a 95-year-old woman with a Taser is guilty of manslaughter
item.description: SYDNEY, Australia (AP) — A police officer who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter in an Australian court Wednesday. A jury found Kristian James Samuel White guilty in the trial in Sydney after 20 hours of deliberation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine police file criminal complaints against VP Sara Duterte and her security aides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine police officials on Wednesday filed criminal complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte and her security staff for allegedly assaulting and disobeying orders from authorities in a recent altercation in Congress.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah start a ceasefire after nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Lebanon-based Hezbollah militants began a ceasefire Wednesday in a major step toward ending nearly 14 months of fighting as a region on edge wondered whether it will hold.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Namibia votes for president and the chance for its first female leader
item.description: A woman who joined Namibia’s independence movement in the 1970s is a strong contender to become its first female leader as the country votes in a presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australian father of teen sextortion victim backs banning young children from social media
item.description: Wayne Holdsworth became an advocate for banning Australian children younger than 16 from social media because his son took his own life after falling victim to an online sextortion scam.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Worst November snowstorm in half century hits Seoul and grounds hundreds of airplane flights
item.description: The worst November snowstorm in half century has hit Seoul, grounding hundreds of lights countrywide and disrupting commuter traffic in the capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Displaced people return to south Lebanon as ceasefire appears to hold
item.description: Long-displaced residents of south Lebanon started returning to their homes amid celebrations hours after a ceasefire between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group took effect early Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon begins
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon’s Hezbollah militants began early Wednesday morning, after Beirut residents endured the most intense day of Israeli strikes since the war began. Many wondered if the agreement to stop fighting would hold.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Childhood cancer patients in Lebanon must battle disease while under fire
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Carol Zeghayer gripped her IV as she hurried down the brightly lit hallway of Beirut’s children’s cancer center. The 9-year-old's face brightened when she spotted her playmates from the oncology ward.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU AL JOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani authorities start clearing Imran Khan supporters from the capital after deadly clashes
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistani security forces launched an operation Tuesday night to disperse supporters of imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan who had gathered in the capital to demand his release from prison.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured in UK after 20 years
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years. San Diego, 46, wanted for his alleged involvement in two bombings in Northern California in the 2000s, was arrested in Wales, the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Biden to make new push for ceasefire in Gaza after Israel-Hezbollah deal
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday said the U.S. will make a new push for a ceasefire in Gaza in the coming days, after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to pause their fighting in and around the border with Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Russian migrants being denied access to asylum process: attorney
item.description: "They are being subjected to a blanket policy that they are applying to Russian asylum seekers," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: 8 arrested in Laos after 6 tourists died from suspected methanol poisoning
item.description: Officials arrested eight hostel workers in Laos on Monday for allegedly poisoning travelers with methanol, according to reports from the Australian Broadcasting Corp. Tourists reported illnesses after consuming alcohol in the town of Vang Vieng, a popular backpacking destination.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau says he had 'good call' with Trump amid tariff threats
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Tuesday he had a “good call” with President-elect Trump following the incoming U.S. leader’s pronouncement that he would impose a 25 percent tariff on Canadian goods when he takes office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Biden announces cease-fire plan between Israel and Hezbollah ending 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced his support for a cease-fire deal that has yet to be finalized between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia expels British diplomat after accusing him of spying
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian authorities on Tuesday ordered a British diplomat to leave the country on allegations of spying that the U.K. dismissed as “baseless” as tensions soar over the conflict in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican cattle imports suspended after New World screwworm detected
item.description: The United States Department of Agriculture’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) announced that a New World screwworm (NWS) was detected in Mexico on Friday, causing the agency to pause animal imports from the country. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A violent showdown in Pakistan's capital pits the government against Imran Khan supporters
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Thousands of people are in the heart of Pakistan’s capital to demand the release of imprisoned former premier Imran Khan. He has been behind bars since August 2023 on charges that he and his supporters say are politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada braces for surge of immigrants hoping to move from US
item.description: Canadian officials will limit how many new permanent residents it allows amid an expected surge at the northern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, The Hill

item.title: South Korean man convicted for deliberately gaining weight to evade military service
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean man has been sentenced to a suspended prison term for deliberately gaining more than 20 kilograms (44 pounds) to evade a tougher role in the country’s military conscription system, a Seoul court said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladeshi court denies bail to Hindu leader who led rallies for minority protection
item.description: A prominent Bangladeshi Hindu leader who has been leading rallies demanding security for Hindus in the overwhelmingly Muslim nation has been ordered detained on charges of sedition.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian forces capture former British soldier fighting for Ukraine in Kursk: report
item.description: A former British Army soldier was reportedly captured by Russian forces while fighting alongside Ukrainian soldiers in the Kursk region of the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two senators urge FIFA not to pick Saudi Arabia as 2034 host
item.description: Democrats Ron Wyden of Oregon and Dick Durbin of Illinois have told FIFA president Gianni Infantino in a letter “we urge you to seek out a host country with a record of upholding human rights.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man in India regains consciousness before his cremation on funeral pyre: reports
item.description: A 25-year-old man who was declared dead in India regained consciousness shortly before he was set to be cremated this week, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists study ‘very rare’ frozen remains of 35,000-year-old saber-toothed cub
item.description: Scientists studied a three-week-old saber-toothed cub dating back 35,000 that had been left almost perfectly intact, thanks to its frozen condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel keeping its ‘eyes open’ for Iranian attacks during Trump transition period, ambassador says
item.description: Israel's Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon speaks to Fox News Digital about the threat from Iran during the Trump transition period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, WGN 9

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation
